steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that highly decorated sets were not common until the 1600s, meaning simple stages were the standard before then. The question asks what this implies about earlier audiences' relationship to simple stages.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — If simple stages were the standard, audiences would expect them—what people regularly experience becomes their norm. ✓
  • Choice B — Implies audiences found simple stages surprising or unusual, which contradicts them being the established standard. ✗
  • Choice C — Suggests audiences were bothered by simple stages, which contradicts that this was normal. ✗
  • Choice D — Implies audiences demanded something different, again contradicting that simple stages were the accepted norm. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
